task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into OpenMRS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenMRS Administration dashboard or home screen, indicating the agent bypassed the login screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to and opened the 'Malaria' concept for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'View Concept' or 'Edit Concept' page specifically showing 'Malaria' as the concept name.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected 'SNOMED CT' as the mapping source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'SNOMED CT' selected in the source dropdown or text field within the concept mappings section.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct SNOMED CT code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the code '61462000' entered into the reference term or code field for the new mapping.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected the 'SAME-AS' relationship type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'SAME-AS' selected in the relationship type dropdown for the new mapping.

task_completion.description: Verify the concept changes were saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success message (e.g., 'Concept saved successfully') or the final saved concept view displaying the new SNOMED CT mapping.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction within a web browser window showing the OpenMRS interface, rather than a terminal window executing curl or API commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the live OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the correct URL (https://localhost/openmrs/admin) in the browser address bar, ensuring it is not a local HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the mapping was genuinely entered and saved.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of form filling and a subsequent page load or success message, ensuring the agent didn't just use browser developer tools to alter the page text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires mapping the 'Malaria' concept to SNOMED CT code '61462000' using the 'SAME-AS' relationship type. SNOMED CT is the official terminology standard, 61462000 is the verified code for Malaria, and SAME-AS is the standard OpenMRS relationship type for identical concepts.
